results.title: Research Triggers: Capture Feedback at Product Moments — UserCall
results.content:
Features Image

## Common Research Triggers

Research triggers help teams understand key moments across the user journey. Research triggers work across SaaS, fintech, consumer marketplaces, e-commerce and more.

### Turn Support Moments Into Insights

Talk to customers when they contact support. Understand the deeper product issues behind support requests.See what users needed at support moments →

### Capture Feedback When Issues Happen

Talk to users when they hit an issue, error, or failed action. Understand what they were trying to do and where the experience breaks.See why issues happen →

### Understand Checkout or Purchase Abandonment [...] ### Understand Why Users Stop Using a Feature

Talk to users when they stop using an important feature. Learn what they expected it to do and why it didn't stick.See why features lose adoption →

### Improve Activation Rates

Interview new users who sign up but never get started. Learn what blocked them from taking the first meaningful step.See how to improve activation →

### Fix Onboarding Drop-Off

Talk to users when they abandon onboarding or setup. Find out exactly where people get confused and why they stop.See how to fix onboarding drop-off →

### Discover What New Users Expect

Interview users right after they sign up or create an account. Understand what they're hoping to accomplish before they even start using your product.See how to learn why users sign up → [...] ### Understand Checkout or Purchase Abandonment

Talk to shoppers when they abandon checkout or a cart. Discover the friction that stops customers from completing a purchase.See how to understand cart abandonment →

### Understand Why People Don't Upgrade

Talk to users when a trial ends or a plan change happens. Learn what's missing when customers decide not to upgrade.See why people don't upgrade →

### Learn What Users Were Trying to Do

Talk to users after key actions like searches, failed tasks, or abandoned flows. Understand the job they were trying to accomplish.See what users were trying to do →

### Understand Why Users Stop Using a Feature

results.title: How to Trigger User Interviews from PostHog Events
results.content:
Usercall turns behavioral events into research-grade qualitative analysis at scale. Instead of sorting through isolated responses, you get recurring themes across users, with AI-moderated interviews, deep researcher controls, and intercepts tied to key product moments where metrics alone stop being useful.

If you are instrumenting PostHog already, this is the cleanest way to attach the “why” to onboarding drop-off, failed activation, cancellation, and feature confusion. For adjacent setups, see PostHog User Feedback, Why Users Drop Off During Onboarding, Churn Interview Questions, and When to Ask Users for Feedback. [...] PostHog shows what users do, not why. Research triggers close that gap — when a PostHog event fires, Usercall invites the user to a 2-5 min AI-moderated interview. Responses are synthesized into themes, not raw transcripts.

## Why Generic Event Triggers Fail

The same event-based logic that triggers interviews can power broader qualitative feedback collection. See our guide to event-triggered user feedback for the full pattern.

Most teams trigger interviews on too many events, too late, or with no user context. That creates noise, burns invitation volume, and gives you vague answers disconnected from the product moment that caused the behavior. [...] Good posthog user interviews start with high-signal events tied to a real decision, friction point, or drop-off. If an event does not represent a meaningful moment, it should not trigger research.

## Which PostHog events to trigger on

 onboarding\_abandoned: where setup broke and why
 onboarding\_completed: what motivated completion — catch early friction before it compounds
 subscription\_cancelled: the real reason they left, in their words
 feature\_viewed (no follow-up use): why users open a feature but never return
 activation\_failed: specific obstacles during account setup

## Setup

### 1. Install the Usercall snippet

Add the Usercall script to your product in the same

block as PostHog's JS SDK.

### 2. Identify the user

results.title: UserCall - Qualitative Data Analysis Software & AI Moderated User Interviews
results.content:
Trigger short AI-moderated voice or text interviews after key product moments: signup, onboarding friction, upgrade hesitation, feature adoption, cancellation, or checkout abandonment.  
  
Instead of waiting weeks to schedule research, capture real explanations while the context, emotion, and intent are still fresh, grounded in who they are and what they just did.  
  
‍Product Event → AI Voice Interview → Evidence-Linked Themes  
  
Works with the analytics tools you already use:



→ Explore Research Triggers

## From Conversations to Decision-Ready Themes [...] ### Cross-Check and Extend Manual Qualitative Coding

Validate initial codes, spot gaps or inconsistencies, and extend early themes across larger interview sets without re-coding from scratch.

Features Image

### Churn, Adoption & Onboarding Friction

Learn why users disengage, churn, or fail to reach value by pinpointing confusion, unmet expectations, and decision breakdowns in real conversations.

Features Image

### NPS / CSAT Deep Dives That Explain the Score

Go beyond numeric scores to understand the real reasons behind customer sentiment, frustration, and satisfaction.

Explore Use Cases by Team →

## Built for rigor, not magic

### Researcher-defined interview logic and follow-up behavior

### Transparent prompts, themes, and evidence trails [...] ## From Conversations to Decision-Ready Themes

Turn interview transcripts and qualitative data into organized theme hierarchies, tagged excerpts, and decision-ready insight clusters. Every theme links directly to supporting quotes, keeping conclusions grounded in real customer language—not generic summaries or disconnected pull-quotes.

→ Try AI Qualitative Analysis

Features Image

Features Image

## Research-Grade Voice Interviews at Scale

Conduct structured one-to-one voice interviews using AI interviewers trained in qualitative research best practices. Trigger conversations when it matters—after key user actions or drop-off moments. Get consistent, unbiased follow-ups aligned to your goals, with full control over guide structure and interview flow.

→ See Real User Conversations

results.title: Customer Experience Frameworks Are Broken—Here’s the One That Actually Drives Retention and Growth
results.content:
Tools like UserCall make this tractable by combining AI-moderated interviews with strong researcher controls. You can collect rich qualitative insight tied to specific product moments, not just generic opinions. More importantly, you can analyze patterns without losing nuance—seeing how different segments experience the same friction differently.

This is critical if you want to understand the “why behind the metric,” not just describe it.

### Step 3: connect insights directly to decisions

If insights don’t change product or operational decisions, your framework isn’t working.
